channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Indiana Pacers
channel.description: Indiana Pacers trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: I think after making a trade like that, you’ve got to …
item.description: Draymond Green: “Listen, I think after making a trade like that, you’ve got to put out a statement like this. This is one of those save-my-ass statements. But I will say, tough luck, man. Very tough luck. You did get your starting center, but if that comes at the heels of a perennial All-Star, unless the Pacers go win a championship, this could be viewed as a top-five worst trade ever. Now, like he said, a little unlucky, but if it does not pan out the right way, this could go bad. And meanwhile

item.title: Kevin Pritchard: If Tyrese Haliburton doesn’t go down, I think we win the title
item.description: Kevin Pritchard: “But when we did this trade, we tried to figure out: if we didn’t get it, are we going to be okay? And we will be okay. We’ve got our top seven or eight coming back. We’ve got some free agency. This team deserves another chance to win it. “Game 7 was awful, but if Ty doesn’t go down, I think we win it. Maybe that’s blustery or whatever, but I really think we win it. “And we could have gotten to this point and had the pick, and still had a gaping hole at five. We couldn’t risk no

item.title: Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as …
item.description: Don't take my word for it. Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as his team's lottery pick (to an extent) when reflecting on the trade. "At the trade deadline, didn't know how structurally we were (going to be) able to get a top center. Then you might be just looking at free agency and using your cap. And we wouldn't have cap space. So it's a full mid level. And that guy usually isn't in the level of (Zubac)," he began before the kicker. "So (Zubac) is our lottery pick."

item.title: Pacers president Kevin Prichard apologies to fans after losing first-round pick: 'I own taking this risk'
item.description: Kevin Pritchard: I'm really sorry to all our fans. I own taking this risk. Surprised it came up 5th after this year. I thought we were due some luck. But please remember - this team deserved a starting center to compete with the best teams next year. We have always been resilient.

item.title: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA Clippers will also receive the 2028 second round Pacers pick AND The 2029 Pacers first round pick. Bennedict Mathurin is a restricted free agent and Isaiah Jackson is under contract through 2028

item.title: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going to the Clippers if the Clippers did not get the 2026 first, which they have now.

item.title: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick …
item.description: Law Murray: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick and will draft FIFTH

item.title: I think after making a trade like that, you’ve got to …
item.description: Draymond Green: “Listen, I think after making a trade like that, you’ve got to put out a statement like this. This is one of those save-my-ass statements. But I will say, tough luck, man. Very tough luck. You did get your starting center, but if that comes at the heels of a perennial All-Star, unless the Pacers go win a championship, this could be viewed as a top-five worst trade ever. Now, like he said, a little unlucky, but if it does not pan out the right way, this could go bad. And meanwhile

item.title: Kevin Pritchard: If Tyrese Haliburton doesn’t go down, I think we win the title
item.description: Kevin Pritchard: “But when we did this trade, we tried to figure out: if we didn’t get it, are we going to be okay? And we will be okay. We’ve got our top seven or eight coming back. We’ve got some free agency. This team deserves another chance to win it. “Game 7 was awful, but if Ty doesn’t go down, I think we win it. Maybe that’s blustery or whatever, but I really think we win it. “And we could have gotten to this point and had the pick, and still had a gaping hole at five. We couldn’t risk no

item.title: Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as …
item.description: Don't take my word for it. Pritchard said himself, later, that he views Zubac as his team's lottery pick (to an extent) when reflecting on the trade. "At the trade deadline, didn't know how structurally we were (going to be) able to get a top center. Then you might be just looking at free agency and using your cap. And we wouldn't have cap space. So it's a full mid level. And that guy usually isn't in the level of (Zubac)," he began before the kicker. "So (Zubac) is our lottery pick."

item.title: Pacers president Kevin Prichard apologies to fans after losing first-round pick: 'I own taking this risk'
item.description: Kevin Pritchard: I'm really sorry to all our fans. I own taking this risk. Surprised it came up 5th after this year. I thought we were due some luck. But please remember - this team deserved a starting center to compete with the best teams next year. We have always been resilient.

item.title: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers aren't done paying for Zubac btw. The LA Clippers will also receive the 2028 second round Pacers pick AND The 2029 Pacers first round pick. Bennedict Mathurin is a restricted free agent and Isaiah Jackson is under contract through 2028

item.title: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going …
item.description: Law Murray: The Pacers keep their 2031 first. That was only going to the Clippers if the Clippers did not get the 2026 first, which they have now.

item.title: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick …
item.description: Law Murray: The LA Clippers have STOLEN the Indiana Pacers pick and will draft FIFTH

item.title: Despite the risk of losing this year’s pick, Pritchard …
item.description: At 29, Ivica Zubac should be in his prime. The Pacers likely needed to pay a premium for his deal, which will pay him just $42.1 million over the next two seasons. Additionally, the rest of the lottery is likely to be dotted with point guards. With Haliburton, the Pacers are set there, so protecting a pick further into the lottery had diminishing value for Indiana. Despite the risk of losing this year’s pick, Pritchard said the trade framework allowed the Pacers to keep most of their future draf

item.title: Metta World Peace: Haliburton returning is going to be …
item.description: As well as getting Haliburton back healthy, what do the Pacers need to do in the offseason in order to get back to the NBA Finals? Metta World Peace: Haliburton returning is going to be a massive help, obviously. Outside of that, I don’t think they need much more because they have Coach Rick Carlisle. Coach Carlisle is really good at getting the best out of what he has. In fact, he’s one of the best at this. You saw that during the team’s run to the NBA Finals last season. On paper, I don’t thin

item.title: “Oh, that's easy,” he said. “Giannis. Giannis is going …
item.description: Antetokounmpo reportedly had one foot in and one foot out this season with the Bucks. That comes to mind when listening to Turner’s podcast comments. “If the plane took off at 2 o'clock, we weren't leaving till 4:30,” said Turner, who left the Pacers last offseason to sign a four-year deal worth more than $100 million with Milwaukee after it waived Damian Lillard. Turner continued: “I'm being so serious, bro. It was crazy, dawg. Guys were an hour late to the plane. It got to the point where I kn

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton: 'I didn’t think Kevin Pritchard had to apologize'
item.description: Did you also think that your team was due a little bit of luck? Tyrese Haliburton: I mean, it was a coin toss. The way I’m looking at these last 12 months for me, dating back from June, I didn’t think we were really due any luck. It’s been a rough 12 months for me. I didn’t think KP had to apologize. They took a calculated risk, and I’m still rolling with the decision. I’m excited to add Zu to our crew, and I think we’re going to be really good alongside him. It’s just how the cookie crumbles so

item.title: “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Donte DiVincenzo says he’s spoken to Jayson Tatum, Tyrese Haliburton and Damian Lillard about the process of coming back from an Achilles. “They’ve all helped me in different ways and all their journeys are different.”

item.title: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points …
item.description: Josh Dubow: Cavs are 2nd team ever to win a Game 7 by 20+ points after losing Game 6 by 20+ points. The Magic did it in 1995 Eastern Conference final, losing Game 6 123-96 at Indiana and winning Game 7 105-81 at home

item.title: The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 …
item.description: Orlando and Memphis split this season's two Europe games in Berlin in London. Berlin and Paris are scheduled to serve as host cities for the two-game tour in 2028. The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 season as its launch date for a 12-to-16 team NBA Europe circuit that would be separate from #thisleague. Wemby and the Spurs played two games against Indiana in Paris in January 2025 on the NBA's last in-season visit to France.

item.title: Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Want to hear something wild? Will Hardy now has the seventh-longest active tenure among all NBA coaches, after Jason Kidd's ouster in Dallas. Erik Spoelstra, Steve Kerr, Tyronn Lue, Mark Daigneault, Chris Finch, Rick Carlisle ... and then Will Hardy.

item.title: “It’s going to be a series, that’s for sure. We spoke …
item.description: What do you take away from Game 1? Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's trainer, Nem Ilic: “It’s going to be a series, that’s for sure. We spoke after the game. They went through it last year two times, both against Denver and Indiana. It’s funny how things work. Once another team wins the first game, then it’s all like, ‘Oh, are they good enough to take down San Antonio? Is [Nikola] Jokić going to beat Shai in the second round last year? Is Indiana going to win the Finals?’ I feel like it gets a little b

item.title: James - the NBA's all-time leading scorer - will be …
item.description: LeBron James will likely have a few things to say about free agency, the state of the NBA and his own future on July 16. James - the NBA's all-time leading scorer - will be appearing at Fanatics Fest NYC that day at the Javits Center in New York and recording a live version of the "Mind the Game" video podcast with Indiana guard Tyrese Haliburton, the sports apparel giant announced Wednesday. The taping will come roughly 2 1/2 weeks after the start of NBA free agency. James has not yet announced

item.title: He actually called back. Steve knew this was his one …
item.description: Alexander began to tell Wilkins the tale: It was March 31, 1984. “I stole Dominique’s home phone number from my girlfriend’s father’s Rolodex,” Alexander said. Her father was a photographer who took photos of NBA players. He wrote down the number on a small sticky note and dialed — because, back then, you could just … call NBA stars. “Hello?” Wilkins said. “Hi. This is Steve. I’m the kid in Indiana who always tries to get your shoes when you’re here.” Each time the Hawks came to Indianapolis to

item.title: Sources told ESPN that last season's loss to the …
item.description: Sources told ESPN that last season's loss to the Indiana Pacers, spoiling a 64-win season, was devastating, with one saying it felt like "getting punched in the face." It was then that they knew they needed that infusion.

item.title: Thursday marks the one-year anniversary of Haliburton …
item.description: Thursday marks the one-year anniversary of Haliburton — still rehabbing from the torn Achilles he suffered in Game 7 of the 2025 NBA Finals — hitting what was ultimately ruled a game-tying shot after review, but still became one of the defining moments of the postseason. Haliburton’s willingness to embrace the moment — and the role of the villain in front of a hostile crowd — did not come by accident. The then fifth-year pro’s showmanship was shaped through years of watching professional wrestli

item.title: “I think I’ve just grown a reputation in our league,” …
item.description: As his basketball career blossomed, he folded those lessons on television into his own persona on the floor. It was why he felt so comfortable leaning into the heel role in that moment at Madison Square Garden and why he doesn’t shy away from being the “bad guy” whenever the Pacers hit the road. “I think I’ve just grown a reputation in our league,” Haliburton told The Athletic earlier this season. “Like when we go on the road — I talk s—, I am who I am, and I think on the road it’s easy to say t

item.title: I think there’s a growing mainstream popularity within …
item.description: Why do you think wrestling has gotten so big? Tyrese Haliburton: I think there’s a growing mainstream popularity within it. It used to be like, I don’t want to say not cool, but everybody goes through the same phase, right? When you’re super young you like it, then you find out John Cena’s not actually dying. He’s not actually so hurt that he can’t move.

item.title: For sure. I welcome it. I feel like part of our sport …
item.description: It’s interesting because so many people remember the Reggie choke after you hit the shot at MSG. In wrestling, there’s a performance … Tyrese Haliburton For sure. It’s performing. Is there a part of that, even looking back at that moment, where you’re like, “Hey, I want all the heat. I want you all to boo the hell out of me because I know what I just did?” Haliburton: (Smiles) For sure. I welcome it. I feel like part of our sport is entertainment, and I think all the best players are great enter

item.title: Andrew Nembhard and Pascal Siakam checkin' in from the …
item.description: Indiana Pacers: Andrew Nembhard and Pascal Siakam checkin' in from the Lucas Oil 500 Festival Parade

item.title: In the 10 games since the non-decision, the lineup …
item.description: In the 10 games since the non-decision, the lineup combos with Mikal Bridges and the Knicks’ two stars, Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns, have a staggering plus-28.6 net rating. New York kept cooking even with a key player, Anunoby, missing two games in the second round; the Knicks won those games, both on the road, by a combined 44 points. From nowhere, in other words, the Knicks are a juggernaut. This outburst was rather unexpected, to say the least. The idea of the Knicks winning the East

item.title: If the Cavaliers lose in 4 or 5, they'll become the …
item.description: Zach Kram: If the Cavaliers lose in 4 or 5, they'll become the 4th conference finalist this century with a losing playoff record. But the silver lining is what happened with the 3 previous teams: - '24 Pacers—made the finals in '25 - '20 Nuggets—won in '23 - '03 Pistons—won in '04

item.title: For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference champion lost in the ECF the year before: 2026: Knicks (lost in '25 ECF) 2025: Pacers (lost in '24 ECF) 2024: Celtics (lost in '23 ECF) 2023: Heat (lost in '22 ECF) (via UnderdogNBA) #NBA

item.title: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: If the Cleveland Cavaliers want a shred of hope, the Knicks are gonna be the fourth straight Eastern Conference finals loser to make the Finals the next year. They follow the Pacers, Celtics and Heat.

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton: "I knew going into the game there …
item.description: Rayasianboy: "If time goes back, Game 7, but you know you're gonna tear your shit. Would you still play?" Tyrese Haliburton: "Sure, we had a chance." Rayasianboy: "You think so? You'll still play? Even if you know you're gonna fuck up, you'll still play?" Tyrese Haliburton: "I knew going into the game there was a chance, so yeah, I wouldn't change it. If I had sat, that would have been weak."

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton: "I would just say, dog, it's okay …
item.description: Tyrese Haliburton: "I would just say, dog, it's okay to say no to people. That's what I really struggled with early in my career, saying no to people because it's hard, especially when it's people you love, people that been there when you need them and stuff." Rayasianboy: "They've been telling you to get the water, be the water boy as a rookie, and you're scared to say no." Tyrese Haliburton: "Those are just rookie duties. You gotta do that. Yeah, you gotta do that." Rayasianboy: "If you don't

item.title: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to …
item.description: If the Finals reaches six games, fans would have to fork up over $4,500 on June 16. It’s a stark contrast to 10 years ago when Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors faced off in the 2016 NBA Finals and the average price to get into those games was $880. Last year, when the Thunder and Pacers clashed, the average ticket price was $1,147, according to TickPick.

item.title: A visual update from Tyrese Haliburton, now 11 months …
item.description: Scott Agness: A visual update from Tyrese Haliburton, now 11 months removed from Achilles surgery.

item.title: Donte DiVincenzo to return during the 2026-27 season?
item.description: It is far too early in the recovery process to predict when Donte DiVincenzo will return to action. Tearing an Achilles tendon is among the most serious injuries in the game, often keeping players out for a year or more. Boston’s Jayson Tatum returned in less than 10 months, but that is considered an outlier. Still, DiVincenzo is trying to follow Tatum’s lead. He flew to New York for surgery less than 24 hours after suffering the injury, following a similar timeline that Tatum went through. DiVi

item.title: The Pacers will hold their first pre-draft workouts in …
item.description: The Pacers will hold their first pre-draft workouts in preparation for the 2026 NBA Draft on Thursday, May 28 and Friday, May 29 at the Ascension St. Vincent Center. The first workout group on Thursday includes Nimari Burnett (Michigan), Tucker DeVries (Indiana), Sam Hoiberg (Nebraska), Emanuel Sharp (Houston), Peter Suder (Miami), and Seth Trimble (North Carolina). The second workout group on Friday includes M.J. Collins (Utah State), Barry Dunning Jr. (Pitt), Nate Johnson (Kansas State), Jaxon

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton on flopping: It's part of player development, it's getting taught before they get into the NBA
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Hali on if coaches are teaching flopping: "Yeah for sure it's being taught. I don't think it's as much head coach teaching as much as it is like player development even before you get to the NBA it's just something you like kind of work on by nature...even in pickup they're working on how they can draw fouls. I think that's a part of the game"

item.title: "A place like New York they have a lot of passionate …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Tyrese Haliburton: "A place like New York they have a lot of passionate fans of course and people love the Knicks, but there's a lot of celebrities in there and there are sometimes they're like too cool to get super loud vs OKC's"

item.title: “Jeremy Lin was a great teammate,” Anunoby said. “I …
item.description: Anunoby, despite being robbed of an opportunity that some players 15 years his senior never got to experience, remembers that time fondly. He looks back and reflects on all that he learned from his teammates about how to be a pro, how to prepare at the sport’s highest level. The players on that team were so influential on who Anunoby is today that he wanted to list the entire roster, as many as he could recall while put on the spot, to show appreciation for what that experience meant to him as h

item.title: Six more prospects are working out for the Pacers …
item.description: ?Alex Golden?: Six more prospects are working out for the Pacers today, and I wrote about all of them. Ernest Udeh Jr - Athletic Big Nate Johnson - Physical Guard Jaxon Kohler - Stretch 4 Kowacie Reeves Jr - 2 Way Wing M.J. Collins - 2 Way Guard Barry Dunning Jr - Wing www.si.com/nba/pacers/o...

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: While athletes in tennis and even the NFL have double …
item.description: While athletes in tennis and even the NFL have double dipped in broadcasting and playing, the trend of active player contributors continues to gain steam in professional basketball. NBA players Kyle Lowry and Tyrese Haliburton joined Prime Video as athlete contributors. On the WNBA side, Kelsey Plum signed on as a player contributor for Prime, Sophie Cunningham is working as a studio analyst for USA Network and Caitlin Clark made multiple appearances on NBC’s studio show for NBA games.

item.title: “Sometimes when older guys are talking about the …
item.description: “Sometimes when older guys are talking about the sport, sometimes it can sound a little, ‘Back in my day,’ whereas I think getting my perspective on things, as a guy who is having success currently, is just a little different,” Haliburton said. “I think it’s interesting to hear their perspective on things and have rebuttals and conversations which I’m appreciative of, because obviously Steve (Nash) and Dirk (Nowitzki) played in a different game versus myself. So I’m very appreciative of those di

item.title: The Indiana Fever have revoked the credential of …
item.description: The Indiana Fever have revoked the credential of longtime Indiana Pacers and Fever beat reporter Scott Agness, he shared on his own Fieldhouse Files website. According to Agness, the Fever notified him via email on May 21 that he would be barred from accessing team events because of a social media report they claim spread “inaccurate and unsubstantiated” information regarding Caitlin Clark’s health status for the season.

item.title: Then, following NBA All-Star Weekend in Indianapolis, …
item.description: This situation also comes against the backdrop of my relationship with the Pacers. Before the start of the 2023-24 season, my access to the team inexplicably shifted overnight — after being the only one to cover every training camp practice — from daily access to weekends only. Then, following NBA All-Star Weekend in Indianapolis, those restrictions increased to no access and I was taken off the email distribution list because I parked in the media lot “without the appropriate passes or clearanc

item.title: One concern raised involved me catching up with a …
item.description: I do not have a clear explanation, but I believe that they do not appreciate my existing personal and professional relationships that I’ve built from being at The Fieldhouse for more than 20 years. One concern raised involved me catching up with a member of the support staff several hours before Game 1 of the NBA Finals. When I was informed that I needed to leave the court area, I did so immediately. Another involved having a conversation with a longtime member of building security in the corner

item.title: I would have been the only Indianapolis media member …
item.description: However, that has now affected my standing with NBA PR as well. I would have been the only Indianapolis media member in Los Angeles last February to cover Pascal Siakam’s fourth All-Star appearance, but that credential request was declined for the first time. The same thing happened last month for the NBA Draft Lottery, despite my radio partner, 107.5 The Fan (the flagship station of the team), wanting to include my coverage in its Monday morning program.

item.title: And for the last few years at NBA Summer League in Las …
item.description: And for the last few years at NBA Summer League in Las Vegas, I was told I was not welcome to cover Pacers practice between games (while another reporter did). Then, they only made one player available postgame despite being required to make two. I cannot definitively say whether those Pacers circumstances influenced the Fever’s decision. What I can say is that this latest action follows a pattern that has made it increasingly difficult for an independent journalist to provide the type of on-the
